constraints: Use PyJWT[cryptography] for JWT validation — NOT python-jose (abandoned library)
JWT middleware must handle both HS256 (SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET) and ES256 (JWKS endpoint) algorithms; algorithm is determined from token header
organization_id MUST be extracted from app_metadata in the JWT claims, never from user_metadata
AsyncSessionLocal must be created with expire_on_commit=False
Application pool must use NullPool and statement_cache_size=0 (Supavisor transaction pooling compatibility)
Alembic migrations must use DATABASE_DIRECT_URL (port 5432); application queries must use DATABASE_URL (port 6543)
AuditEvent ORM model must expose no update() or delete() methods; only Session.add() for inserts
RLS UPDATE policies must have a corresponding SELECT policy; both must use auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' ->> 'organization_id'
transition_case_status handler is the single authoritative gatekeeper for all status transitions — no module may bypass it
Closed-case guard must execute before handler logic to prevent partial writes before rejection
